title: Structural tier classifier misclassifies cross-cutting infrastructure as CONSUMER
description: CEU-EXTRAS was classified as CONSUMER tier by the structural CEU derivation pipeline, but architecture documentation (application-registry.md, AGENTS.md, models.md feature matrix) reveals it provides cross-cutting infrastructure that all domain apps depend on. The structural classifier uses import patterns (IMPORT_DOMINANT) to determine tier, but extras has high OUTBOUND imports (consuming from many apps) AND provides infrastructure (registry, mixins) that every app inherits. Import direction alone is insufficient for tier classification of cross-cutting infrastructure components.
enrichment_guidance: Future CEU derivation should consider: (1) whether an app provides model feature mixins consumed by other apps, (2) whether an app hosts application-wide registries, (3) whether external documentation classifies it as infrastructure vs domain. Import direction analysis should be supplemented by inheritance provision analysis for cross-cutting apps.

title: Architecture documentation not consumed as evidence type
description: The generalized evidence intake pipeline (PI.PIPELINE.GENERALIZED-EVIDENCE-INTAKE.01) extracts APP_CONFIG, MODEL_INVENTORY, URL_NAMESPACE, OFFICIAL_DOCS, MODEL_DOCS evidence types. It does NOT extract: ARCHITECTURE_DOCS (development guides, application registry docs, model feature matrices), FEATURE_DOCS (feature-level cross-cutting documentation), REPO_ARCHITECTURE (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README architecture sections). These documentation types contain structurally relevant evidence about cross-cutting patterns, architectural intent, and dependency architecture that code-graph analysis alone cannot reveal.
enrichment_guidance: Future evidence intake should consider extracting: (1) docs/development/ for architecture documentation, (2) docs/features/ for cross-cutting feature documentation, (3) AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md for official repository architecture descriptions. These are complementary to code-graph evidence — they reveal intended architecture vs actual structure.

title: Import-only authority pattern misses inheritance-dominant behavioral authority
description: CEU-TENANCY was classified with IMPORT_DOMINANT authority pattern, but structural evidence (40.3c centrality) shows inheritance authority (226 cross-domain edges) at 2.22x its import authority. The dual structural authority hero moment identified this discrepancy, but the CEU derivation pipeline only records one authority_pattern. Cross-cutting behavioral frameworks that primarily influence through mixin inheritance (forms, tables, filtersets) are underrepresented by import-only authority classification.
enrichment_guidance: CEU derivation should compute both import_authority_score and inheritance_authority_score, then classify as DUAL_AUTHORITY when the ratio exceeds a threshold (e.g., inheritance >= 1.5x import). The DUAL_AUTHORITY_CROSS_CUTTING pattern is structurally distinct from DUAL_AUTHORITY (which currently means balanced bidirectional coupling).

title: Spine objects summary section does not auto-update on append
description: spine_objects.json summary section reports 14 total objects (8 evidence, 5 hero) but actual content is 16 (9 evidence, 6 hero). The summary was computed at generation time and was not updated when objects were appended post-generation. This creates a consistency gap between the summary section and the actual object arrays. A consumer reading only the summary would undercount spine objects.
enrichment_guidance: Spine object append operations should recompute the summary section. Alternatively, consumers should count array lengths directly rather than trusting the summary. Pipeline should include a spine_objects consistency check in its validation phase.

title: Evidence ingestion selection requires human judgment without persistent workflow
description: The Phase 7 evidence ingestion step required selecting which documentation sources to ingest. This selection was made through Claude-session analysis (reading docs, identifying structurally relevant content). The selection reasoning is captured in the evidence_ingestion_delta artifact but the selection PROCESS is not a persistent governed workflow. A future operator or session could not reproduce the selection without re-analyzing the documentation.
enrichment_guidance: Evidence ingestion should evolve toward: (1) an evidence source registry that catalogs available documentation, (2) a selection workflow that records which sources were considered and why, (3) a persistent recommendation engine that suggests evidence sources based on existing CEU gaps.

description: Proposition SP-netbox-0074 spans 6 CEUs without specific per-CEU evidence
Proposition SP-netbox-0075 spans 12 CEUs without specific per-CEU evidence

title: AST-based authority extraction is feasible, high-fidelity, and immediately actionable for NetBox
description: Python AST inheritance extraction from 896 NetBox source files (excluding migrations) yields 1,494 categorized authority edges across 6 Django framework categories (MODEL, VIEW, FORM, SERIALIZER, FILTERSET, TABLE) covering all 12 CEUs with zero parse errors. This is 5.6x-52.5x richer than topology_report inheritance counts for most CEUs. The extraction enables converting AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions from DERIVED (uniform 0.704) to DIRECT_EVIDENCE with per-CEU categorized authority composition. The probe script (authority_probe.py) is sufficient — no general-purpose code graph engine, SCIP integration, or call graph extraction is needed.
enrichment_guidance: Build authority enrichment pipeline: (1) run AST extractor on canonical_repo, (2) persist categorized edges as authority_edges.json, (3) feed into SPE AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY deriver as DIRECT_EVIDENCE, (4) produce per-CEU authority composition propositions. NetBox-specific (Django patterns) — parameterize for framework detection but do NOT generalize prematurely.

title: Two CEUs lack AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions despite having authority edges
description: CEU-EXTRAS (286 authority edges across 6 categories) and CEU-UTILITIES (25 edges across 3 categories) have framework authority inheritance but no current AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions — they were not included in SPE's authority topology derivation. CEU-EXTRAS is particularly significant: 286 edges make it the second-highest authority CEU after CEU-DCIM (444), yet it has zero authority propositions. This is a coverage gap in the semantic proposition corpus, not an extraction limitation.
enrichment_guidance: When authority enrichment pipeline is built, ensure all 12 CEUs receive AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions proportional to their authority edge evidence. CEU-EXTRAS and CEU-UTILITIES should not be excluded from authority derivation.

title: AST authority enrichment converts all 10 AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions from DERIVED to DIRECT_EVIDENCE
description: Authority enrichment pipeline consumed 1,494 categorized AST edges to enrich 10 existing AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions and add 2 new ones (CEU-EXTRAS, CEU-UTILITIES). All 12 propositions now at DIRECT_EVIDENCE tier with variable confidence (0.72-0.95, mean 0.841, was uniform 0.704). Two discrepancies fixed: CEU-DCIM and CEU-TENANCY authority_pattern labels contradicted their dominant_axis. Corpus DIRECT_EVIDENCE ratio improved from 57/75 to 69/77. Confidence is evidence-proportional, not inflated — CEU-UTILITIES (25 edges, 3 categories) gets 0.72 while CEU-DCIM (444 edges, 6 categories) gets 0.95.
enrichment_guidance: AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY is now the strongest evidence-backed class after COUPLING_PATTERN. Next enrichment targets: HERO_MOMENT_GROUNDING (6 DERIVED/NOVEL at 0.598 mean) — 2 propositions reference dual authority topology that enrichment strengthens, but full reconciliation is a separate stream.

title: Two AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions had authority_pattern labels contradicting their own dominant_axis
description: SP-netbox-0049 (CEU-DCIM) and SP-netbox-0052 (CEU-TENANCY) both had authority_pattern=IMPORT_DOMINANT but dominant_axis=inheritance in their structural_refs. The proposition text and derivation_rationale correctly described inheritance dominance, but the pattern label was wrong. AST enrichment corrected both to FULL_STACK_VIEW_DOMINANT based on categorized authority evidence. Root cause: SPE's authority topology derivation assigned patterns from a fixed rule set that did not account for post-reconciliation refinements. CEU-TENANCY's rationale noted 'Authority pattern refined during reconciliation' but the pattern field was not updated.
enrichment_guidance: Future SPE runs should verify consistency between authority_pattern, dominant_axis, and derivation_rationale. Pattern assignment should be a final step after all refinements.
